headline: Petronas Dividend to Government Falls RM10B Short
context: Petronas declared a RM32 billion dividend to the federal government for 2025, RM10 billion below the RM42 billion budgeted, as global oil prices averaged USD62 per barrel against the USD75 assumption.

cards.big: RM10 billion shortfall from Petronas forces emergency mid-year budget revision across 8 ministries
cards.sub: Petroleum income funds 22% of federal revenue — every USD10 oil price drop costs RM8 billion

cards.big: Malaysia's petroleum revenue dependency at 22% is highest among non-OPEC Asian economies
cards.sub: Norway saves 80% of oil revenue in sovereign fund — Malaysia saves effectively zero

cards.big: Education Ministry absorbs RM2.3 billion cut — affecting 1,200 planned rural school upgrades
cards.sub: Development spending for Sabah and Sarawak cut 18% due to revenue shortfall rebalancing

cards.big: Petronas governance framework allows government to demand up to 90% of profits as dividend
cards.sub: National oil company's reinvestment rate at 28% of profits is below Shell's 45% and Aramco's 35%

cards.big: Treating Petronas as a government ATM leaves nothing for the day the oil wells run dry
Should Malaysia cap Petronas dividends at 50% of profits and channel the rest into a heritage fund?
